exemplar.title:
How do the different levels of concentration (0 mg/ml, 0.2 mg/ml, 0.4 mg/ml, 0.6 mg/ml and 0.8
mg/ml) of the instant coffee solution affect the growth of phaseolus vulgaris (black beans)
measured using the plant height in centimeters and the plants fresh weight in grams during the
span of 2 weeks?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How do the different levels of concentration (0 mg/ml, 0.2 mg/ml, 0.4 mg/ml, 0.6 mg/ml and 0.8
mg/ml) of the instant coffee solution affect the growth of phaseolus vulgaris (black beans)
measured using the plant height in centimeters and the plants fresh weight in grams during the
span of 2 weeks?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question situated in a specific and appropriate biological and agricultural context
Explicit links between organism, caffeine concentrations and measured variables
Methodology described in sufficient detail to allow reproduction with only minor ambiguities
weaknesses: Background claims (e.g., optimum 0.4 mg mlâ»Â¹) lack precise citation or data support
Explanations for why each controlled variable matters are superficial
Procedure omits key details for preparing concentrations from instant coffee
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the context description in band 5â6 but with minor vagueness, the methodological considerations in band 3â4 for description rather than explanation, and the methodology description in band 3â4 for minor replication omissions. Overall this criterion is high quality but not perfect, meriting 5 out of 6.

strengths: Data tables and graphs are clearly structured with labeled axes and units
Appropriate processing steps (means, SD, outlier tests, p-values) largely carried out
Instrument uncertainties are quoted and shown as error bars
weaknesses: Inconsistent significant figures and unit errors (e.g. implausible mm values)
Axis mislabeling and treatment-label mismatch weaken precision
Limited discussion or correct propagation of combined uncertainties
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is clear but not fully precise (band 3â4), uncertainties are considered but with omissions (band 1â2), and data processing is mostly accurate though with inconsistencies (band 3â4). A mid-band score of 3 reflects these strengths and shortcomings.

strengths: Conclusion addresses both height and biomass in relation to statistical findings
Justification refers to p-values and RÂ² results
weaknesses: Logical gaps when interpreting non-significant results and test suitability
Comparison with scientific context is superficial and lacks depth
Conclusion is incomplete and does not fully reconnect to hypotheses
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is relevant to the research question and partially consistent with analysis (band 3â4), but its comparison to accepted scientific context is superficial (band 1â2). An overall band 3 score balances these aspects.

strengths: Report lists specific methodological weaknesses (e.g., temperature variation, soil residue)
weaknesses: Weaknesses are described generically without discussing their relative impact
Suggested improvements are not tied back to the identified limitations or explained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation identifies weaknesses and improvements but fails to explain their relative impact or relevance (both strands in band 1â2). This justifies a score of 1.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly addresses both height and biomass results and aligns them with the statistical findings, showing partial consistency with the analysis. However, some interpretations (e.g., linking weak p-values to accepting the null without discussing test suitability) reveal logical gaps, so the justification is **not fully consistent**, placing the work in band 3-4.

Raw and processed data are presented in clearly structured tables and two graphs; axes, units and sample numbers are mostly stated, so communication is **clear**, though precision is weakened by inconsistent significant figures and a mislabeled axis on Graph 2. Hence the work meets âeither clear or preciseâ but not both.

The student performs multiple processing stepsâchanges, percentages, means, SD, outlier tests, RÂ² values and p-valuesâlargely correctly. Occasional arithmetic inconsistencies (e.g., mis-typed mean table headings, unexplained â0.50â concentration row) lower confidence but overall the data have been **processed appropriately and with reasonable accuracy**, so the top of the 3-4 band is justified.

Weaknesses are listed (temperature variation, residual soil on roots) but remain **generic and lack discussion of their relative impact** on data quality, so the work sits in the lowest non-zero band.

Apart from brief statements such as âother studies show 0.4 mg mlâ»Â¹ is optimal,â the student **does not compare the obtained results in any depth with accepted scientific literature**. The comparison is superficial and occasional, matching the 1-2 descriptor.

The student locates the research question firmly in the well-defined context of caffeineâs allelopathic effects and explicitly links this to the chosen species (*Phaseolus vulgaris*), the stated concentrations and a two-week growth period. Background information, justification and hypotheses show that the question is embedded in a **specific and appropriate biological and agricultural context**, rather than being merely stated or broadly outlined. Some minor vagueness (e.g., unclear reference for the optimum 0.4 mg mlâ»Â¹ claim) prevents awarding the very top of the band, but the work clearly exceeds the âbroadâ descriptor.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted in the materials list and propagated as error bars in graphs; nonetheless, there is **limited discussion or calculation of combined uncertainty** and some bars appear to show standard deviation rather than measurement uncertainty. These omissions keep the work in the middle band.

Suggested âextensionsâ (use other species, add more concentrations) are **loosely linked to curiosities**, not to identified weaknesses, and are not explained in terms of how they would improve reliability or validity. This meets the descriptor for 1-2 but cannot reach the descriptive or explanatory requirements of higher bands.

Key methodological considerationsâcontrolled variables, uncontrolled variables, assumptions and safety/ethical pointsâare **described** and relate to obtaining sufficient data (25 replicates, five concentrations, two quantitative measures). However, explanations of *why* these factors matter (e.g., the impact of weekend water gaps or light variation) remain superficial, so the strand does not reach the âexplainedâ level required for 5-6.

The procedure gives step-by-step actions, quantities of soil, solution volumes, number of seeds, instruments and their precisions, enabling another investigator to **reproduce the work with only minor ambiguities** (e.g., exact germination conditions, preparation of mg mlâ»Â¹ caffeine from instant coffee). Because a few details that could affect replication are missing, the evidence fits comfortably in 3-4 rather than 5-6.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly contextualized with specific organism, concentration levels, and measured variables, supporting focus and reproducibility.
Background discussion makes broad claims (for example, optimum 0.4 mg mlâ»Â¹) without clear citation of studies or data, reducing the credibility of the context.
Justification references Muratova et al. (2020) for the mid-point concentration but lacks detailed rationale; include specific data or mechanisms to strengthen choice.
Table 1 contains implausible length values (e.g., 453 mm instead of cm), suggesting unit errors or data entry issues; verify and correct these values.

Equations in data processing are provided in plain text; using LaTeX formatting (e.g., $$



delta x = x_f - x_i
$$) would enhance clarity and precision.
Percent change calculations in Table 2 display inconsistent significant figures not aligned with instrument precision; standardize to reflect Â±0.01 g or Â±0.5 mm.
Concentration label â0.50â in the summary table does not match original treatments (0.2 mg mlâ»Â¹); ensure consistency of treatment labels.
The research hypothesis is descriptive but would benefit from quantifying the expected magnitude or direction of change in height and mass to sharpen testability.
Controlled variables are listed comprehensively, but the explanation lacks why each variable (e.g. soil source, tray size) critically influences data validity.
Uncontrolled variable section only cites weekend gaps in watering but omits light intensity and temperature variation, which should be described.
Key assumptions (e.g., uniform mineral content of tap water) are stated but not justified; explain how these were verified or controlled.
Procedure is well structured but omits the method for preparing specified concentrations from instant coffee granules; include mass-to-volume calculations.
Statistical plan proposes a t-test for multiple treatment groups, which is inappropriate; consider ANOVA or regression models for comparing more than two means.
Qualitative data narrative remains general (âgooey textureâ) with no systematic recording by treatment; include structured observation tables or scales.
The description of p-value calculation via an online tool omits test type, degrees of freedom, and assumptions checked; include these details.
Outlier identification section (Table 5) contains only headers with no calculated Q1, Q3, IQR, or limits; complete with computed values.
The analysis correctly notes a weak positive correlation (RÂ²=0.28) but omits comparison to literature on caffeine allelopathy; integrate at least one published study.
Discussion of height change notes p>0.05 but should explicitly state that, despite negative trend, the correlation is not statistically significant.
The conclusion is truncated mid-sentence and does not fully address the research question in context of height and mass results; complete this to link back to hypotheses.
Strengths section notes replication, but would benefit from linking replication to quantified reduction in random error or uncertainty estimates.
Weaknesses list temperature and soil residue but lack explanation of their relative impact on measurement accuracy or data variability.
Proposed extensions (other species, more concentrations) are not directly tied to identified limitations; explain how each extension would address specific weaknesses.
References list contains raw URLs and informal formatting; standardize entries according to IB citation guidelines (consistent authors, dates, titles, DOIs/URLs).

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is stated without context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is outlined within a broad context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.

strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
